title: The Use of Primary Hepatocytes in Assessment of Drug Safety and Toxicity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"w9r5z","text":"The identification of biomarkers for toxicity is becoming increasingly important for drug discovery and development. This chapter describes the preparation and utilization of primary rat hepatocytes as a cellular model of steatosis. A protocol is presented for dosing the cells with the steatosis-inducing compound amiodarone, along with the conduction of assays for measuring lipid accumulation and mitochondrial function. A differential solubility extraction procedure is also presented, which can be used for proteomic profiling analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"oz2qh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
